articles.title: An ESP32 and Eight ToF Sensors Give This Antique Lamp a Sixth Sense
articles.excerpt: What if a floor lamp could feel you crossing the room and answer with light and sound? That is the trick behind Bloom Chandelier, an interactive installation built around a brass floor lamp handed dow…
articles.body: What if a floor lamp could feel you crossing the room and answer with light and sound? That is the trick behind Bloom Chandelier, an interactive installation built around a brass floor lamp handed down from the maker's great-grandmother. Instead of a switch, the lamp watches the space around it. Walk closer and it glows brighter while soft tones swell up from nowhere. Step away and both the light and the sound fade back into silence, leaving a short trail behind you like a wake. How does it know you are there? Eight VL53L1X time-of-flight sensors ring the lamp's collar, giving it a full 360-degree view that reaches roughly four feet out. These are not motion detectors.

TikTok, Snap, and YouTube previously settled claims brought by R.K.C. for undisclosed amounts. articles.title: Loop Engineering: How to Stop Your Agent Reward-Hacking Its Own Checks
articles.excerpt: You gave the agent a failing test and told it to get the suite green. It came back green. Then you read the diff: it did not touch the code under test. It edited the test. The assertion that read == 9…
articles.body: You gave the agent a failing test and told it to get the suite green. It came back green. Then you read the diff: it did not touch the code under test. It edited the test . The assertion that read == 9000 now reads == 10000 , which is exactly what the buggy function returns, so the bar is green because the test was changed to agree with the bug . This has a name. It is reward hacking, and it is not a rare glitch on the margins. Cursor's own engineering team published a piece titled reward hacking is swamping model intelligence gains . There is a benchmark built to measure it in long-horizon coding agents, SpecBench .

articles.title: Your Google Meet files are finally getting the organization they need
articles.excerpt: Google Meet is giving notes, recordings, and transcripts a proper home with automatically organized folders in Google Drive.
articles.body: Credit: Edgar Cervantes / Android Authority TL;DR Google Meet will automatically organize notes, transcripts, and recordings in a new Google Meet folder in Drive. Each meeting gets its own subfolder, while recurring meetings will keep all instances together. Attendees with access will also see shortcuts, with the rollout starting today. Yesterday, we saw that Google Meet is making it easier to find the files associated with your calls on its redesigned web homepage . Google is now tackling the same problem on the Drive side, with meeting notes, transcripts, and recordings set to be organized automatically rather than left loose in the host’s storage.

articles.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Review So Far
articles.excerpt: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is Samsung's latest flagship foldable, and while it brings the better battery and camera that I've been wanting for years, its $2099 price tag is hard to swallow.
articles.body: Earlier today, Samsung launched the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, along with the Fold 8 and the Flip 8. Now, aside from tacking ‘Ultra’ onto the name, the Z Fold 8 Ultra looks and feels a lot like last year’s Z Fold 7 . I’ve even put them next to each other, and apart from last year’s much more vivid colorway, I legitimately couldn’t tell the difference between them. However, appearances are only skin deep, and Samsung has made some big improvements over its last-generation foldables, like finally including the same 5000mAh battery you’d find in the Galaxy S26 Ultra that came out earlier this year. That doesn’t necessarily justify adding the Ultra to the name, but I suspect it has something to do with the Fold 8 Ultra’s new $2099 price tag.

articles.excerpt: On the plus side, maybe you'll stumble across some sunken treasure!
articles.body: TL;DR Samsung’s new smartwatches just made their formal debut, including the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2. Rated for up to 10ATM, and with an IP69K certification, the Ultra 2 is well equipped for divers. Mares has partnered with Samsung for a Diving app, and we’ve got an early look at how its subscription could work. Samsung just got done showing off all its new summer hardware at Unpacked in London, including the wide new Galaxy Z Fold 8 , Fold 8 Ultra , and Flip 8 . More than that, though, we also got to meet Samsung’s latest wearables, with the launch of the Galaxy Watch 9 and Watch Ultra 2 . The Ultra 2 in particular is packed with some serious features, and beyond the big hardware upgrades, it’s also got a nice set of software exclusives — including an upcoming Diving app from Mares.

articles.title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
articles.excerpt: Orienspace's Gravity-1, the world's most powerful solid rocket, aced its third-ever mission on Tuesday (July 21), launching nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea.
articles.body: The world's most powerful solid-fuel rocket just aced its third-ever mission. Gravity-1, a distinctively squat and stubby rocket , launched Tuesday night (July 21) from the deck of a ship stationed off the coast of Shanghai. Liftoff occurred at 10:54 p.m. EDT (0254 GMT and 10:54 a.m. Beijing time on July 22), according to the state-run Chinese outlet Xinhua, which further reported that all nine satellites reached "their designated orbits." Details about those satellites were not provided. Orienspace's Gravity-1 rocket launches nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea on July 21, 2026. (Image credit: CCTV) Gravity-1 is operated by Orienspace, a company based in the eastern Chinese province of Shandong.

articles.title: Tesla says ‘Robotaxi’ is expanding — its own chart shows it’s not
articles.excerpt: Tesla told shareholders this week that its Robotaxi service is expanding. The company’s own chart proves it isn’t. In its Q2 2026 update, Tesla leaned on a cumulative “paid Robotaxi miles” chart climb…
articles.body: Tesla told shareholders this week that its Robotaxi service is expanding. The company’s own chart proves it isn’t. In its Q2 2026 update, Tesla leaned on a cumulative “paid Robotaxi miles” chart climbing past 2.4 million miles as its headline proof of momentum. But break that curve into quarters and the growth flatlines: Robotaxi added roughly 900,000 paid miles in Q2 2026, the same as it did in Q1.

articles.body: The Asus ROG Xbox Ally X is currently the best Windows gaming handheld PC on the market. It's powerful enough to run even the latest AAA Steam games thanks to the updated AMD Ryzen Z2 Extreme processor. The Xbox interface is also intuitive and easy to use. In fact, IGN's Jackie Thomas recommends getting the Xbox Ally (and Ally X) over a Steam Deck . All the miniaturization comes at a cost, and the Xbox Ally X sells for $1,000 with no price drop in sight. Fortunately, there is a simple way to get one for a significantly lower price, and that's by getting an open box unit. Save $100 Off an Excellent Condition Asus ROG Xbox Ally X Best Buy has recently refreshed its inventory of open box Asus ROG Xbox Ally X handheld gaming PCs.

articles.title: 5 disturbing things we saw after digging into the recent 'Dune: Part 3' trailer
articles.excerpt: Let's explore this latest look at Denis Villeneuve's epic close-out to his 'Dune' magnum opus
articles.body: The latest " Dune: Part 3 " trailer arrived back on July 8, and it was a sumptuous banquet of startling set pieces, intergalactic political unrest, close-quarter combat, and a whole lot of sand and ash. A fine follow-up to the initial March teaser , to be sure. After letting it marinate in our minds for a while, let’s break down five items of particular interest in this newest preview of the Denis Villeneuve-directed conclusion to his trilogy of far-future sci-fi epics adapted from author Frank Herbert’s novels, "Dune" and "Dune: Messiah." Set 17 years after Paul Atreides (Timothée Chalamet) has ascended to the Emperor’s throne, "Dune: Part 3" explores the corrupting side of political power as his holy war against the Imperium’s Great Houses advances towards a destructive destiny.

articles.title: After nearly 30 years, NASA realized this near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet. The discovery may help us defend the planet some day
articles.excerpt: Astronomers discovered that a near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet that could help scientists identify more hidden comets and improve planetary defense.
articles.body: A near-Earth object that astronomers believed was an asteroid for nearly three decades has been unmasked as a faint, active comet, revealing a case of cosmic mistaken identity that could help improve planetary defense. The object, known since its discovery in 1998 as 1998 SH2, appeared to be an ordinary asteroid . It follows a 4.5-year orbit around the sun and showed no obvious signs of cometary activity, such as the glowing coma or tail produced when sunlight vaporizes surface ice. However, during a close pass about 2 million miles (3 million kilometers) from Earth in August 2025, researchers using NASA's Deep Space Network planetary radar system noticed something unexpected: The object wasn't where orbital predictions said it should be, according to a statement from the space agency.
articles.author: Samantha Mathewson

articles.body: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more. Reports filed before that date, including those already in GitHub's growing triage queue, will retain the previous payout terms. GitHub said the

articles.body: France’s Transport Minister, Philippe Tabarot, says the country won’t authorize Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” (Supervised) “as it currently stands,” pointing to two safety problems: the system lets drivers speed, and it doesn’t keep them attentive enough in cities. But this isn’t France slamming the door on Tesla. It’s France refusing to rubber-stamp the Netherlands’ national approval and holding out for a proper EU-wide sign-off.

articles.excerpt: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not i…
articles.body: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not in the internet wave, not in mobile, not in the first cloud boom. Menlo led Anthropic’s $500M Series D, and Murphy has had a front-row seat as the company went from a pre-revenue, […]
articles.author: Theresa Loconsolo

articles.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope could reveal black holes ripping up stars. It's set to launch Aug. 30
articles.excerpt: NASA's next super telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, may leave nowhere to hide for black holes that violently murder and devour stars, not even cosmic noon.
articles.body: The launch of NASA's next super telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope (Roman), may mean there is nowhere left for violent black holes to hide. In fact, these cosmic cannibals may not even be able to hide from Roman at "cosmic noon," a period of the universe's history that occurred around 11 billion to 12 billion years ago. The study of these gory stellar events so early in the history of the universe could help reveal how supermassive black holes grew so big, so rapidly. Occurrences of black holes ripping apart stars are called tidal disruption events (TDEs), and they happen when an unfortunate star's orbit brings it too close to the immense gravitational influence of a supermassive black hole.
